ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for mango-coconut-sago dessert drink specifically. FOODON_03315763 (coconut milk beverage) is the closest defensible parent. A more specific term covering fruit-sago-coconut milk drinks does not exist in FoodOn as of 2026-04.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (coconut milk based beverage) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for mango-coconut-sago dessert drink as of 2026-04.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (coconut milk) is used as the closest parent anchor for this coconut-milk-based dessert drink. No specific SNOMED CT concept for mango-coconut-sago drink exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g (one cup), as typically served in Singaporean bubble tea and dessert drink outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Mango sago coconut milk dessert drink — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Mangos raw (FDC ID 169910), Coconut milk canned (FDC ID 170172), Tapioca/sago pearls cooked (FDC ID 169717), and added cane sugar; weighted blend at approximate proportions 40% mango pulp, 40% coconut milk, 15% cooked sago, 5% sugar syrup
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific branded product (HeyTea Coconut Mango Boom). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted blending of USDA FoodData Central entries for the four primary components at approximate proportions reflecting a typical mango-coconut-sago dessert drink: 40% fresh mango pulp, 40% canned coconut milk, 15% cooked sago pearls, 5% sugar syrup. This is a medium-confidence estimate. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a matching entry for this specific product. FAO/INFOODS SEA data for mango and coconut milk were cross-checked and are consistent with USDA values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mango and coconut milk components — analogous estimate based on: Mango GI ~51 (Atkinson FS et al., 2008, International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care); Coconut milk GI ~41 (estimated low per University of Sydney GI Database fat-dominant food convention); Sago pearls GI ~70 (Atkinson FS et al., 2008); weighted blend at 40:40:15:5 proportions yields blended GI ~55
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HeyTea Coconut Mango Boom. GI estimated by weighted blending of component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed tables: mango GI 51 (40% weight), coconut milk GI ~41 (40% weight), cooked sago GI 70 (15% weight), sugar syrup GI 65 (5% weight). Blended GI = (51×0.40)+(41×0.40)+(70×0.15)+(65×0.05) = 20.4+16.4+10.5+3.25 = 50.55, rounded to 51; adjusted upward to 55 to account for real-world sugar addition and serving variability. GI 55 is classified as 'low' (borderline medium). GL per 400 g serving = (55 × 51.2 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 28.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~18 mg per 100 g; ~72 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of this drink is low at approximately 72 mg per standard 400 g serving, posing minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, individuals with hypertension should be mindful of the added cane sugar and total caloric load if consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (borderline 55), high sugar content (~9.2 g per 100 g), and high glycemic load per serving (~28.2)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 28 per standard serving and approximately 36.8 g of sugar per 400 g serving, this drink poses a high glycaemic burden for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be consumed only occasionally, with preference for the no-sugar-added option where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (~4.6 g per 100 g; ~18.4 g per 400 g serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content derived from coconut milk is approximately 18.4 g per standard 400 g serving, which substantially exceeds the recommended daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and consider requesting reduced coconut milk or smaller portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Mango Sago Dessert Drink (branded as 'Coconut Mango Boom' by HeyTea) is a chilled Southeast Asian-style dessert beverage made from fresh mango pulp, rich coconut milk, and cooked sago pearls, sweetened with cane sugar. The drink is served cold and has a creamy, tropical flavour profile with the natural sweetness and slight tartness of ripe mango balanced by the richness of coconut milk and the soft, chewy texture of sago pearls. Sago pearls are small translucent starch spheres derived from the sago palm, similar in appearance and texture to tapioca pearls used in bubble tea. This style of dessert drink is popular across Singapore, Hong Kong, and Southeast Asia, often served in bubble tea shops and dessert cafes. Nutritionally, the drink is moderately energy-dense at approximately 98 kcal per 100 g, with a significant contribution from coconut milk fat (predominantly saturated fat at ~4.6 g per 100 g) and from natural and added sugars (~9.2 g per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low, borderline medium), and the glycemic load per standard 400 g serving is approximately 28, making it a high-glycaemic-load beverage that warrants caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The drink provides modest amounts of vitamin C (~12 mg per 100 g) and vitamin A (~38 mcg RAE per 100 g) from the mango component. The product description notes that a sugar-free sweetener option may be available; consumers managing blood glucose or weight should request the no-sugar-added variant. The product is noted to contain dairy products per the vendor description, though the primary fat source is coconut milk.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific branded product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted blending of USDA FoodData Central component entries (mango, coconut milk, sago, sugar) at approximate proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed tables. Medium confidence is appropriate as the estimation methodology is defensible and cross-checked against FAO/INFOODS SEA data, but exact product formulation proportions are not publicly disclosed by HeyTea.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (FDC IDs: mango raw 169910, coconut milk canned 170172, tapioca/sago cooked 169717, cane sugar) at approximate proportions 40% mango, 40% coconut milk, 15% cooked sago, 5% sugar syrup. Exact HeyTea formulation is not publicly available. 2. The product description states it contains dairy products; however, the primary ingredients listed are mango, coconut milk, and sago. The dairy presence may relate to cross-contamination or a minor dairy-based ingredient not disclosed in the public menu description. is_dairy_free is set to false accordingly. 3. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as coconut milk and mango contain negligible industrially produced trans fats; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data. 4. cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 as plant-based ingredients (mango, coconut milk, sago) contain no dietary cholesterol; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is set to 0.0 as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived B12 sources; naturally zero. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is set to 0.0 as none of the primary ingredients (mango, coconut milk, sago) are significant sources of vitamin D; naturally zero. 7. GI estimated at 55 (low, borderline medium) by weighted blending of component GI values from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008; GL per 400 g serving = 28.2. 8. Monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 0.2 g from USDA coconut milk and mango data). Polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 0.1 g. 9. The product is sold at SGD 8.50 at HeyTea Marina Bay Sands, Singapore. 10. Sugar-free sweetener option noted as potentially available per vendor description; nutritional values here reflect the default cane sugar version. 11. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dessert drink conventions: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi mandatory per G6. 12. Sentinel value -1 is NOT used for any field in this record; all micronutrient values are estimated from USDA component data and are numeric.
